Develop impeccable taste in the age of AI
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Thumbnail by AuthorWith AI generating interfaces on demand and beginner designers using those outputs as references, what makes you stand out is your taste.
Every designer I’ve watched develop quickly had one thing in common. They were all inspired by great work, and that has always been how taste gets built.
What taste actually is
Taste is your judgment. The choices you make for the work in front of you.
What font, layout, and pattern. What color, spacing, what to emphasise, and what to strip away. Every decision you make while designing is an expression of taste, and the quality of those decisions is what separates work that feels considered from work that feels assembled.
A cleaner way to think about it: taste has three layers.
The first is surface.
That immediate reaction when you look at something and know it works, or know something is off, before you can explain why. This is the layer most people think of when they say a designer “has taste.” It comes with time and repeated exposure to good work.
The second is context.
Good taste reads the room. A choice that works beautifully for one brand, one audience…
